task:
Add brownfield project support to claw-forge (/tmp/claw-forge). Full spec in PLAN.md which the Planner must write first.

## What brownfield support means
claw-forge currently only works on greenfield (new) projects. It must also work on existing codebases to:
1. Understand the existing codebase (analyze command)
2. Add new features respecting existing patterns (add command)
3. Fix bugs with reproduce-first protocol (fix command)

## Planner must produce PLAN.md covering:

### Documentation first (write these files as part of PLAN.md output)
- docs/brownfield.md — full user-facing guide: how brownfield mode works, all commands, workflow diagrams, examples
- Update README.md brownfield section
- Update ARCHITECTURE.md with brownfield components

### New components to design
1. BrownfieldAnalyzer (claw_forge/plugins/analyzer.py)
   - Scans existing project: language, framework, conventions, test baseline
   - Reads git log --stat to find hot files (most changed = business logic)
   - Detects code style: naming conventions, error handling patterns, logging approach
   - Outputs brownfield_manifest.json

2. brownfield_manifest.json schema
   - stack: {language, framework, package_manager, test_runner}
   - conventions: {naming, error_handling, logging, imports}
   - test_baseline: {framework, total_tests, passing, coverage_pct}
   - hot_files: [{path, change_count, role}]
   - entry_points: [{type, path, description}]
   - architecture: {layers, patterns, key_modules}

3. claw-forge analyze command
   - Runs BrownfieldAnalyzer
   - Writes brownfield_manifest.json to project root
   - Prints summary: stack, test count, hot files

4. claw-forge add <feature> command
   - auto-runs analyze if no brownfield_manifest.json
   - Creates git branch feat/<slug>
   - Adds single feature to feature queue
   - Runs with brownfield-aware agent prompts (read manifest first, match patterns, dont break tests)
   - Runs existing tests before and after

5. claw-forge fix <description> command
   - auto-runs analyze if no brownfield_manifest.json
   - Creates git branch fix/<slug>
   - Specialized bug-fix flow:
     a. Write failing test that reproduces bug (RED)
     b. Find root cause (systematic-debug skill)
     c. Surgical fix — minimum code change
     d. Confirm failing test now passes (GREEN)
     e. Run full test suite — must be green
   - Generates fix report

6. Enhanced agent system prompts for brownfield
   - Read brownfield_manifest.json before any code changes
   - Run existing tests first to establish baseline
   - Match existing code conventions (from manifest)
   - Never change files not related to the task
   - Branch-first always

7. New CLI commands wired into claw_forge/cli.py

### Tests to plan
- tests/test_analyzer.py — BrownfieldAnalyzer unit tests (mock git, mock filesystem)
- tests/test_brownfield_commands.py — CLI command tests

### Constraints for Builder
- Line length <= 100 (ruff)
- Full type annotations
- No new dependencies (use subprocess for git, pathlib, json)
- All existing 557+ tests must still pass
- New code >= 90% coverage
- Git: Alex Chen / alex.chen31337@gmail.com
